title: shorea helferi
intro: Shorea helferi, also known as the Helfer's Shorea, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The bark of Shorea helferi has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Shorea helferi include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin infections, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Shorea helferi may include decoctions of the bark, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application for skin conditions.
